title: Rajasthan Basic Computer Instructor: Job Profile, Eligibility, Salary, and Career Guide
excerpt: A complete guide to the Rajasthan Basic Computer Instructor role, including eligibility, responsibilities, salary, selection process, career growth, and required skills.
content:
The Rajasthan Basic Computer Instructor is a government teaching position responsible for providing computer education in government schools across Rajasthan. The role focuses on developing students' digital literacy, teaching computer fundamentals, and introducing practical computer applications.

Job Responsibilities

  • Teach computer fundamentals and digital literacy.
  • Conduct practical computer lab sessions.
  • Train students in MS Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint).
  • Teach internet usage, email, and cyber safety.
  • Maintain computer lab equipment and software.
  • Prepare lesson plans, assignments, and practical examinations.
  • Maintain attendance, student records, and performance reports.
  • Support online learning initiatives introduced by the Rajasthan Government.

Eligibility

  • B.E./B.Tech in Computer Science, Information Technology, Electronics, or equivalent.
  • BCA, MCA, M.Sc. (Computer Science/IT), or equivalent qualifications as specified in the official notification.
  • Knowledge of Hindi (Devanagari script) and Rajasthan culture is generally required.

Selection Process

  • Written Examination.
  • Document Verification.
  • Final Merit List.

Salary

The salary is based on the Rajasthan Government pay structure. During the probation period, candidates receive a fixed remuneration. After successful completion of probation, they receive salary as per the applicable Pay Matrix along with allowances such as Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA), and other government benefits.

Required Skills

  • Computer Fundamentals
  • MS Office Suite
  • Programming Basics
  • Networking Fundamentals
  • Internet and Cyber Security
  • Teaching and Communication Skills

Career Growth

With experience, instructors may receive departmental promotions, salary increments, and opportunities to take on senior educational or administrative responsibilities according to Rajasthan Government service rules.

This position offers stable government employment, regular salary progression, pension-related benefits under applicable rules, paid leave, and a balanced work-life schedule, making it a popular career option for computer graduates in Rajasthan.
author: Admin

title: Rajasthan RAS Exam 2026: Complete Guide to Eligibility, Syllabus, Exam Pattern, Salary, Promotion & Preparation Strategy
excerpt: Planning to appear for the Rajasthan RAS Exam 2026? This comprehensive guide covers everything you need to know, including eligibility criteria, exam pattern, detailed syllabus, selection process, salary structure, job profile, promotion opportunities, preparation tips, and frequently asked questions.
content:
Rajasthan RAS Exam 2026: Complete Guide to Eligibility, Syllabus, Exam Pattern, Salary, Promotion & Preparation Strategy

The Rajasthan Administrative Service (RAS) Examination is one of the most prestigious and competitive state-level civil service examinations conducted by the Rajasthan Public Service Commission (RPSC). Every year, thousands of aspirants compete for administrative positions in various departments of the Rajasthan Government. Selected candidates become part of the state's administrative machinery and contribute to governance, law enforcement, public welfare, revenue administration, and policy implementation.

This guide provides complete information about the RAS Exam 2026, including eligibility, syllabus, exam pattern, salary, promotion opportunities, preparation strategy, and career prospects.

What is Rajasthan RAS?

The Rajasthan Administrative Service (RAS) is the highest state civil service in Rajasthan. Officers recruited through this examination are appointed to various State Services and Subordinate Services under the Rajasthan Government.

After selection, officers receive training and are posted in different districts across Rajasthan. Their responsibilities include implementing government policies, maintaining law and order, managing public administration, and ensuring efficient delivery of government services.

Posts Offered Through RAS

Successful candidates may be appointed to several prestigious posts, including:

  • Rajasthan Administrative Service (RAS)
  • Rajasthan Police Service (RPS)
  • Rajasthan Accounts Service
  • Rajasthan Commercial Tax Service
  • Rajasthan Cooperative Service
  • Rajasthan Industry Service
  • Rajasthan Food & Civil Supplies Service
  • Rajasthan Planning Service
  • Rajasthan Employment Service
  • Rajasthan Tourism Service
  • Rajasthan Transport Service
  • Rajasthan Jail Service
  • Rajasthan Excise Service
  • Rajasthan Women & Child Development Service
  • Various Subordinate Services

Eligibility Criteria

Educational Qualification

Candidates must possess a Bachelor's Degree in any discipline from a recognized university.

Final-year students may also be eligible if they fulfill the conditions specified in the official RPSC notification.

Age Limit

  • Minimum Age: 21 Years
  • Maximum Age: 40 Years

Age relaxation is provided to reserved categories according to Rajasthan Government rules.

Nationality

Applicants must be citizens of India.

Selection Process

The Rajasthan RAS Examination consists of four stages:

  1. Preliminary Examination
  2. Main Examination
  3. Personality Test (Interview)
  4. Document Verification

The Preliminary Examination is qualifying in nature, while the final merit is prepared based on marks obtained in the Main Examination and Interview.

RAS Preliminary Exam Pattern

ParticularDetails
Exam ModeOffline (OMR Based)
Question TypeMultiple Choice Questions
Total Questions150
Total Marks200
Duration3 Hours
Negative MarkingAs per RPSC rules
LanguageHindi & English

The Preliminary Examination serves only as a screening test.

RAS Main Examination Pattern

The Main Examination consists of four descriptive papers.

Paper I

General Studies I – 200 Marks

Paper II

General Studies II – 200 Marks

Paper III

General Studies III – 200 Marks

Paper IV

General Hindi & General English – 200 Marks

Interview

  • 100 Marks

Total Marks

  • Mains: 800 Marks
  • Interview: 100 Marks
  • Grand Total: 900 Marks

Detailed Prelims Syllabus

The Preliminary Examination covers a wide range of subjects including:

  • History of Rajasthan
  • Ancient, Medieval and Modern Indian History
  • Indian National Movement
  • Geography of Rajasthan
  • Geography of India
  • World Geography
  • Indian Constitution
  • Political System
  • Governance
  • Rajasthan Economy
  • Indian Economy
  • Agriculture
  • Science & Technology
  • Environment
  • Current Affairs
  • Sports
  • Awards & Honours
  • Computer Awareness
  • Reasoning
  • Mental Ability

Detailed Mains Syllabus

The Main Examination is descriptive and focuses on analytical understanding.

Major topics include:

  • Rajasthan History & Culture
  • Art & Architecture
  • Indian Heritage
  • World History
  • Constitution of India
  • Public Administration
  • Ethics & Integrity
  • International Relations
  • Rajasthan Administration
  • Governance
  • Economic Development
  • Agriculture
  • Disaster Management
  • Science & Technology
  • Environmental Issues
  • Internal Security
  • Current Affairs
  • Essay Writing
  • General Hindi
  • General English

Salary Structure

Selected RAS Officers receive salary according to the Rajasthan Government Pay Matrix.

During Probation

Candidates receive a fixed monthly remuneration as prescribed by the Government.

After Probation

After successful completion of probation, officers receive salary under the applicable Pay Matrix along with government allowances.

Approximate Monthly Salary

  • Basic Pay: ₹56,100 (Level-14 Pay Matrix)
  • Dearness Allowance (DA)
  • House Rent Allowance (HRA)
  • Special Allowances
  • Travel Allowance (TA)
  • Medical Benefits

The gross monthly salary generally ranges between ₹75,000 and ₹1,10,000+, depending on the place of posting and applicable allowances.

Additional Benefits

RAS Officers receive several government benefits, including:

  • Government Accommodation (subject to availability)
  • Official Vehicle (for eligible posts)
  • Pension and retirement benefits as per applicable rules
  • Medical reimbursement
  • Paid leave
  • Job security
  • Promotion opportunities
  • Government training programmes

Job Responsibilities

A Rajasthan RAS Officer performs various administrative duties such as:

  • Implementing government policies
  • Revenue administration
  • Disaster management
  • Public grievance redressal
  • Development project monitoring
  • Election duties
  • Maintaining law and order (in coordination with police)
  • Administrative inspections
  • District-level planning
  • Coordination among government departments

Promotion Opportunities

RAS offers excellent career growth.

Typical promotion hierarchy includes:

  1. Rajasthan Administrative Service Officer
  2. Sub Divisional Magistrate (SDM)
  3. Additional Collector
  4. District Collector
  5. Divisional Commissioner
  6. Secretary to Government
  7. Principal Secretary
  8. Additional Chief Secretary

Outstanding officers may also be inducted into the Indian Administrative Service (IAS) through promotion based on seniority, service record, and availability of vacancies under the prescribed rules.

Preparation Strategy

To perform well in the RAS Examination:

  • Read NCERT books thoroughly.
  • Study Rajasthan-specific history, geography, economy, and culture.
  • Follow daily current affairs.
  • Practice previous years' question papers.
  • Attempt regular mock tests.
  • Improve answer-writing skills for the Mains examination.
  • Revise consistently.
  • Prepare concise notes for quick revision.

Best Books for RAS Preparation

  • NCERT Books (Class 6–12)
  • Rajasthan GK by Lakshya Publications
  • Laxmikanth – Indian Polity
  • Spectrum – Modern History
  • GC Leong – Geography
  • Ramesh Singh – Indian Economy
  • Lucent General Knowledge
  • Rajasthan Economic Review
  • Rajasthan Budget & Survey
  • Previous Year RPSC Question Papers

Frequently Asked Questions

Is the RAS Preliminary Exam qualifying?

Yes. The Preliminary Examination is only a screening test. Final merit is based on the Main Examination and Interview.

Can engineering graduates apply?

Yes. Candidates with a Bachelor's degree in any discipline from a recognized university are eligible.

Is there negative marking?

Yes. Negative marking is applicable as per the latest RPSC examination rules.

Can RAS officers become IAS officers?

Yes. Eligible RAS officers may be promoted to the Indian Administrative Service (IAS) based on seniority, performance, and vacancies under the prescribed promotion process.

Conclusion

The Rajasthan RAS Examination is among the most sought-after government examinations in the state. It offers a respected administrative career, attractive salary, excellent promotion prospects, and the opportunity to contribute meaningfully to public administration. Success in the examination requires disciplined preparation, a strong understanding of Rajasthan-specific topics, consistent practice, and regular revision.
author: Manoj Das Swami
